formal_definition: A sovereign political entity recognized by the international system, identified by a unique name or code. Includes historical states, successor states, and dependencies where coded.

A standardized identifier — numeric or alpha-3 string — that uniquely identifies a country or political entity within a given coding system (COW, Polity, G&W, ISO 3166-1). Different coding systems assign different codes to the same real-world entity, and divergences are especially common for successor states, breakaway territories, and reunified countries.
